recentResults.highlight: Jadeja 43*(29) rescues RR to 159; Jofra Archer 3/20, Nandre Burger 2/27 defend lowest IPL 2026 total — LSG's 4th straight loss, RR jump to 2nd
Sanju Samson 101*(54) — first El Clasico without Dhoni or Rohit; Akeal Hosein 4/17, Noor Ahmad 2/19, Ghazanfar 2/? — MI's BIGGEST defeat in IPL history (by runs), CSK climb to 5th
Kohli 81(44) PoM (dropped on 0) & Padikkal 55(27) stitch 115; Sai Sudharsan 100(58) in vain — Holder 23*(10), late fireworks for GT; RCB climb to 2nd

HIGHEST SUCCESSFUL CHASE IN T20 HISTORY — KL Rahul 152*(67) PoM (1st Indian to 150 in IPL) & Rana 91 set 264; Prabhsimran 76, Iyer 71*(36); Karun Nair drops Iyer twice, Ngidi taken to hospital after head injury
Sooryavanshi 103(37) — 2nd-fastest IPL ton ever, 12 sixes; Ishan Kishan 74(31) PoM & Abhishek 57(29) put on 132 in 55 balls; Cummins returns as captain, takes 2/30 — SRH 4th win in a row

upcomingFixtures.venue: Bharat Ratna Shri Atal Bihari Vajpayee Ekana Cricket Stadium, Lucknow
Maharaja Yadavindra Singh International Cricket Stadium, Mullanpur

storylines.title: T20 record shattered — PBKS chase 265 to stay unbeaten, KL Rahul's 152 in vain
storylines.body: Punjab Kings produced the highest successful run chase in T20 cricket history at the Arun Jaitley Stadium on April 25, hunting down 265 with 7 balls to spare. KL Rahul's masterful unbeaten 152 off 67 (his 6th IPL century, fastest fifty since 2021) — earning him Player of the Match despite being on the losing side, and making him the first Indian batter to score 150 in IPL — combined with Nitish Rana's 91 off 44 (a 28-run over off Bartlett included) had powered DC to 264/2, the highest team total of IPL 2026. But Prabhsimran Singh (76 off 26) and Priyansh Arya (43) gave PBKS a 132-run powerplay-plus opening stand. Karun Nair then dropped Shreyas Iyer twice in successive overs, and the captain made DC pay with an unbeaten 71 off 36. PBKS now sit 13 points clear at the top, unbeaten 6-0-1 in 7 matches. Lungi Ngidi was stretchered off and taken to hospital after a horrific head fall trying to catch Arya. Three of IPL 2026's top totals have all come this week.

storylines.title: Sooryavanshi's 37-ball ton — 2nd-fastest IPL century ever, 12 sixes against the same bowler who got him out last time
storylines.body: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, all of 15 years old, scored his second IPL hundred — 103 off just 37 balls — at Sawai Mansingh Stadium on April 25. He now owns the second AND third-fastest IPL centuries in history (his own 35-ball ton in 2025 still leads). Twelve days after Praful Hinge dismissed him for a duck on debut, Sooryavanshi opened by hitting Hinge for 0, 6, 6, 6, 6 in the first over — 25 runs. Twelve sixes in 37 balls broke RR's franchise record for most sixes in an innings. Fewest innings to four T20 hundreds: Sooryavanshi at 26, next-best Usman Khan at 33. But it went in vain — SRH chased 229 with 9 balls remaining, the second 200-plus chase of the day. Abhishek Sharma (57 off 29) and Ishan Kishan (74 off 31, PoM) put on 132 in 55 balls.

storylines.title: KL Rahul's 152 catapults him to #2 in Orange Cap — DC opener now joint-second on 357
storylines.body: KL Rahul's unbeaten 152 off the DC innings in Match 35 was the highest individual score of IPL 2026, surpassing Abhishek Sharma's 135*. He now sits joint-2nd in the Orange Cap with 357 runs in 7 matches at an average of 59.50 — tied with Sooryavanshi but ahead of Heinrich Klaasen (349) and Virat Kohli (328). Rahul became the fastest to reach 100 in this game (47 balls), surpassed Dhoni in IPL all-time runs, and brought up his 6th IPL century. With Abhishek Sharma still leading on 380, the Orange Cap is now a four-way race between SRH's openers, RR's teenager, and DC's veteran — with Kohli lurking close behind.

storylines.title: End of an era — Cummins' return ends IPL's first all-Indian captains streak after 35 matches
storylines.body: When IPL 2026 began on March 28, all 10 franchises took the field with Indian captains for the first time in league history — Iyer (PBKS), Parag (RR), Patidar (RCB), Kishan (SRH stand-in), Gaikwad (CSK), Axar (DC), Gill (GT), Hardik (MI), Pant (LSG), and Rahane (KKR). That milestone era ended today, April 25, when Pat Cummins returned for SRH after recovering from a lumbar stress injury sustained during the Adelaide Ashes Test in mid-December. Cummins won the toss immediately on return — SRH's first toss win of IPL 2026 (Kishan had lost all 7 he contested). He bowled a peach of a yorker to dismiss RR captain Riyan Parag for 7 off 9, and finished with 2/30 in 4 overs alongside crucial death-overs work. Kishan, freed from captaincy duties, smashed 74 off 31 to win Player of the Match as SRH chased 229 to make it four wins in a row, climbing to 3rd. The 35-match all-Indian-captains chapter is now part of IPL history.

storylines.title: RCB jump to 2nd as Kohli's 81 trumps Sai Sudharsan's century at Chinnaswamy
storylines.body: Royal Challengers Bengaluru chased down 206 with 7 balls to spare on April 24, beating Gujarat Titans by 5 wickets to climb to 2nd in the table. Sai Sudharsan's elegant 100 off 58 (his 3rd IPL ton, fastest to 2000 IPL runs in 47 innings) had powered GT to 205/3, with Holder finishing with an unbeaten 23 off 10. But Kohli — dropped on 0 by Washington Sundar at midwicket off Siraj's first over — punished GT with 81 off 44 and a 115-run partnership with Devdutt Padikkal (55 off 27). Padikkal hit 6 sixes and 2 fours in his 27-ball cameo. RCB have now won 4 of 5 at Chinnaswamy this season; with Kohli grabbing the temporary Orange Cap that night, IPL 2026's batting race continues to swing with every match.

storylines.title: Lungi Ngidi taken to hospital — head injury during DC vs PBKS chase casts shadow over record-breaking day
storylines.body: Amid the highest successful chase in T20 history, Lungi Ngidi was the day's grimmest story. The DC pacer ran back to take a steepler from Priyansh Arya off Axar Patel in the 4th over of PBKS's chase, lost his footing, fell on his back and banged his head heavily on the hard Arun Jaitley outfield. Ngidi was down for several minutes, an ambulance was called onto the field, and a neck brace was applied before he was stretchered off. He was taken to hospital for further examination. South African teammates David Miller and Tristan Stubbs looked visibly shaken; head coach Hemang Badani and Ricky Ponting were involved in the on-field assessment. Dushmantha Chameera came in as the substitute fielder. Latest reports say Ngidi was responsive and conscious — but the IPL community will await further updates.

auctionHighlights.note: Most expensive overseas buy in IPL history — 27(13) in KKR's only win vs RR; cramps have limited his bowling spells
Sri Lanka slinger — calf/hamstring injury from T20 World Cup has kept him sidelined most of the season
Joint record uncapped buy — UP all-rounder picked from base Rs.30 lakh after 5-team bidding war
Joint record uncapped buy — 19yo Rajasthan wicket-keeper batter, serial six-hitter (28 sixes in 12 T20s)
England all-rounder — SRH on a 4-match winning streak, climb to joint-3rd at 10 points after Cummins' return
Australia wicket-keeper — LSG languishing 9th with 5 losses in a row and the tournament's worst NRR
J&K pacer — DC's impact-sub regular, opened the bowling vs PBKS in the record chase
Leg-spinner — RR slip to 4th at 5-3, only got 1 over today as Sooryavanshi's brilliance went in vain
Australian all-rounder — PBKS 6-0-1 with 13 points after T20 record chase, key all-round contributor
South Africa opener — 112*(60) vs PBKS was his peak; golden duck vs CSK continues MI's batting slump
Left-arm pacer — 4/24 vs GT matched his IPL-best, a quiet bargain buy in MI's bowling attack
West Indies all-rounder (back after 2 years) — debut innings of 23*(10) at SR 230 vs RCB; took the Kohli wicket on his return
